message:
fix(stream): replace assert-based completion guards with cached-result pattern

Stream combinators (AnyFuture, AllFuture, CountFuture, ForEachFuture,
DebouncedStream) previously used a bare `completed: bool` flag that
panicked via assert on re-poll. This is unsound in executors that catch
panics and retry polling.

Replace with `result: Option<T>` (or equivalent) so that re-polling a
completed future returns the cached result or a PolledAfterCompletion
error instead of panicking:

- any_all.rs: AnyFuture/AllFuture store Option<bool>, return cached
  bool on re-poll instead of assert!(not completed)
- count.rs: CountFuture stores Option<usize> in `result` field,
  returns cached count; field renamed count→total for clarity
- for_each.rs: ForEachFuture/TryForEachFuture use Option<()> and
  Option<Result> respectively to cache terminal state
- debounce.rs: DebouncedStream guards poll_next with completed flag,
  returns Poll::Ready(None) on re-poll after stream exhaustion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
